title: Phase 2: Advanced Visualization, Engineering Logic & Media Export

requirements.summary: Implement sheeting and cladding system with texture mapping and skylight transparency mode
acceptanceCriteria: Roof and wall surfaces display Trapezoidal or Standing Seam cladding textures based on selected toggle
Skylight mode renders cladding as semi-transparent polycarbonate material
Cladding toggles in the UI control visibility independently for roof and walls

file_operations.description: Create a React Three Fiber component that renders roof and wall cladding meshes using texture mapping. Load the generated trapezoidal texture (frontend/public/assets/generated/cladding-trapezoidal.dim_512x512.png) and standing seam texture (frontend/public/assets/generated/cladding-standing-seam.dim_512x512.png) via useTexture from @react-three/drei. Support toggle-controlled profile selection ('Trapezoidal' or 'Standing Seam') and a Skylight/Polycarbonate mode that sets material opacity to 0.3 with a tinted blue-green color. Apply textures to roof and wall plane geometries using UV mapping. Accept props for roof/wall visibility, profile type, and skylight mode.

Integrate the CladdingSystem component into the 3D scene. Add state for cladding visibility (roof/wall toggles), selected profile type ('Trapezoidal' or 'Standing Seam'), and skylight mode. Pass building geometry dimensions and toggle states as props to CladdingSystem. Ensure cladding renders on top of the structural frame without z-fighting.

Create a UI control panel component with three toggle switches: 'Roof Cladding', 'Wall Cladding', and 'Skylight Mode', plus a radio group or dropdown to select between 'Trapezoidal' and 'Standing Seam' profiles. Use shadcn/ui components (Switch, RadioGroup, Label). Emit state changes to the parent BuildingSimulator component. Style consistently with the existing industrial dark theme.
Wire the CladdingControls panel into the ProjectViewer page UI, positioning it alongside the existing TimelineControls. Ensure the controls are visible and accessible when viewing a project.

requirements.summary: Auto-generate Z-Purlin and C-Girt secondary structural members using InstancedMesh
acceptanceCriteria: Z-Purlins appear across all roof bays at regular intervals using InstancedMesh
C-Girts appear across all wall bays at regular intervals using InstancedMesh
Toggle in UI shows/hides secondary members
No significant frame-rate drop when secondary members are visible

file_operations.description: Create a React Three Fiber component that generates Z-Purlins and C-Girts using InstancedMesh for high performance. Accept building dimensions and bay spacing parameters as props. Compute purlin positions along roof grid lines at regular intervals (derived from bay spacing). Compute girt positions along wall grid lines similarly. Use cylindrical or box geometry for each member type with appropriate cross-section dimensions. Render two separate InstancedMesh instances (one for purlins, one for girts). Support a visibility toggle prop.

Integrate the SecondaryMembers component into the 3D scene. Add state for secondary members visibility toggle. Pass building parameters (bay spacing, dimensions) to SecondaryMembers. Ensure secondary members render behind cladding in the scene hierarchy.
Add a 'Show Secondary Members' toggle switch to the cladding/structure controls panel. Wire the toggle to update the secondary members visibility state in BuildingSimulator.

requirements.summary: Add parametric 3D accessory components (turbo ventilators, gutters, doors, X-bracing) to the scene
acceptanceCriteria: Turbo ventilators appear on ridge and rotate continuously via useFrame animation
Gutters and downspouts are placed along all eave edges
Rolling shutters and personnel doors can be placed on wall grid intersections
X-bracing cross members are rendered in selected wall and/or roof bays
Each accessory type has an independent toggle in the UI

file_operations.description: Create a React Three Fiber component that renders a parametric turbo ventilator using cylinder and cone geometries. Implement a continuous spinning animation using useFrame hook (rotate around Y-axis). Accept position prop for placement on the ridge line. Use metallic material to match building aesthetics.

Create a React Three Fiber component that renders rainwater gutters along eave lines and downspouts at regular intervals. Use extruded tube or box geometries for gutters and cylinders for downspouts. Accept building dimensions and eave positions as props. Use a dark metallic material.

Create a React Three Fiber component that renders rolling shutters and personnel doors. Accept a list of placement positions (snapped to wall grid intersections). Use box geometries with appropriate dimensions for each door type. Implement visual snap-to-grid feedback. Support adding/removing doors via a placement mode.

Create a React Three Fiber component that renders X-bracing rods on selected wall and roof bays. Accept a list of bay indices where bracing should appear. Render crossed cylindrical rods using line or cylinder geometries. Use a contrasting steel color material.

Integrate all accessory components (TurboVentilator, GuttersAndDownspouts, DoorsAndShutters, XBracing) into the 3D scene. Add state for each accessory toggle and placement data (door positions, bracing bay selections). Pass building geometry data to each component. Ensure accessories render at appropriate z-order relative to structure and cladding.

Create a UI control panel with toggle switches for Turbo Ventilators, Gutters/Downspouts, Doors/Shutters, and X-Bracing. Add door placement controls (button to enter placement mode, grid position selector). Add bracing bay selectors (checkboxes or multi-select for bay indices). Use shadcn/ui components and match the industrial theme. Emit state changes to BuildingSimulator.

Wire the AccessoryControls panel into the ProjectViewer page, positioning it in an accessible location alongside other controls. Ensure all accessory controls are reachable without obscuring the 3D viewport.

requirements.summary: Add environment with skybox and ground plane with switchable textures
acceptanceCriteria: Sky background with clouds is visible in the 3D scene
A ground plane is rendered under the building
UI control allows switching ground texture between Concrete, Asphalt, and Grass

file_operations.description: Create a React Three Fiber component that renders the scene environment. Use the Sky component from @react-three/drei to render a procedural sky with visible clouds (adjust sunPosition and turbidity props for cloud effect). Render a large ground plane mesh beneath the building using PlaneGeometry. Load the three generated ground textures (frontend/public/assets/generated/ground-concrete.dim_512x512.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/ground-asphalt.dim_512x512.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/ground-grass.dim_512x512.png) via useTexture. Accept a selectedGroundTexture prop ('Concrete', 'Asphalt', or 'Grass') and apply the corresponding texture to the ground plane material with appropriate repeat settings.

Integrate the Environment component into the 3D scene before other building components. Add state for selected ground texture type. Pass the ground texture selection to the Environment component. Ensure the ground plane is positioned below the building origin.

Create a UI control component with a radio group or dropdown to select the ground texture type (Concrete, Asphalt, Grass). Use shadcn/ui components (RadioGroup or Select). Emit the selected texture type to BuildingSimulator. Style consistently with other control panels.
Wire the EnvironmentControls panel into the ProjectViewer UI, grouping it logically with other scene controls.

requirements.summary: Create calculateBuildingStats utility for steel weight, sheeting area, and load simulation
acceptanceCriteria: Steel weight is calculated correctly from member dimensions and density
Sheeting area subtracts door/opening areas from total surface
Load simulation returns 'Safe' or 'Requires Validation' based on provided inputs
Function is importable and used in the Engineering panel UI

file_operations.description: Create a utility module that exports calculateBuildingStats function. Accept building data including all structural member dimensions (columns, rafters), total roof surface area, total wall surface area, door/shutter opening areas, wind speed (km/h), seismic zone (I–V), and live load (kN/m²). Compute steel weight estimate as: sum of (volume of each member × 7850 kg/m³). Compute sheeting area as: (roof surface area + wall surface area) - door/opening areas. Implement load simulation using simplified IS 800 / MBMA heuristics: if wind speed > 150 km/h OR seismic zone >= IV OR live load > 2.0 kN/m², return 'Requires Validation'; otherwise return 'Safe'. Return an object with steelWeight (kg), sheetingArea (m²), and designStatus ('Safe' | 'Requires Validation'). Include TypeScript types for inputs and outputs.

requirements.summary: Add Engineering/BOQ panel with calculated stats and CSV download functionality
acceptanceCriteria: Engineering panel displays steel weight, sheeting area, and design status
Download BOQ button triggers a CSV file download in the browser
CSV includes columns: Part Name, Quantity, Dimensions, Unit Weight, Total Weight
Design status badge is visually distinct: green for Safe, amber/red for Requires Validation

file_operations.description: Create a UI panel component that displays engineering statistics and BOQ download. Accept building data and engineering inputs (wind speed, seismic zone, live load) as props. Call calculateBuildingStats utility to compute stats. Display steel weight (kg), sheeting area (m²), and design status with a color-coded badge (green for Safe, amber/red for Requires Validation). Include a 'Download BOQ' button that generates a CSV file listing all structural members (columns, rafters, purlins, girts) and cladding parts with columns: Part Name, Quantity, Dimensions, Unit Weight (kg), Total Weight (kg). Use the browser download API to trigger CSV file download with filename 'BOQ_ProjectName_Date.csv'. Use shadcn/ui components (Card, Badge, Button) and match the industrial theme.

Create a utility function that accepts building data (structural members, cladding areas, quantities) and returns a CSV string. For each part type (columns, rafters, purlins, girts, roof cladding, wall cladding), compute quantity, dimensions string, unit weight, and total weight. Format as CSV with header row and data rows. Include totals row at the end. Export a function generateBOQCSV that returns the CSV string.

Create a form component for entering engineering calculator inputs: Wind Speed (km/h) as a number input, Seismic Zone (I–V) as a select dropdown, and Live Load (kN/m²) as a number input. Use shadcn/ui form components (Input, Select, Label). Emit input values to the parent component (ProjectViewer or BuildingSimulator). Style consistently with other panels.

Wire the EngineeringPanel and EngineeringInputsForm into the ProjectViewer page. Add state for engineering inputs (wind speed, seismic zone, live load) with default values. Position the engineering controls in a collapsible sidebar or tab panel. Pass building data and input values to EngineeringPanel. Fetch and pass stored engineering inputs from the backend project record if available.

requirements.summary: Create BuildingSignage component rendering extruded 3D text on the front fascia
acceptanceCriteria: Signage text is visible as 3D extruded geometry on the front fascia
Text updates in real-time as user types in the input field
Signage color follows the 'Trim' RAL color selection
Component can be toggled on/off

file_operations.description: Create a React Three Fiber component that renders 3D extruded text geometry using Text3D from @react-three/drei or Three.js ExtrudeGeometry with a loaded font. Accept props for text string, position (centered on front gable face), and color (from Trim RAL selection). Use a bold sans-serif font (e.g., Helvetica or IBM Plex Sans Bold). Set extrusion depth to create visible 3D effect. Apply material color based on trim RAL color prop. Position the text mesh at the calculated front fascia center point based on building dimensions.

Integrate the BuildingSignage component into the 3D scene. Add state for signage visibility toggle and text content. Pass signage text, position (calculated from building geometry), and trim color to the BuildingSignage component. Ensure signage renders in front of the fascia without z-fighting.

Create a UI control panel for branding settings. Include a text input field for signage text (max 50 characters), a toggle switch to show/hide signage, and integration point for RAL color pickers (to be wired in REQ-9). Use shadcn/ui components (Input, Switch, Label). Emit signage text and visibility state to BuildingSimulator. Style with the industrial theme.

Wire the BrandingControls panel into the ProjectViewer page, positioning it in a dedicated 'Branding' section or tab. Load stored branding settings (signage text) from the backend project record and initialize the form. Save branding changes to the backend when the user updates settings.

requirements.summary: Add RAL Color Picker panel with four independent color selections
acceptanceCriteria: Four separate RAL color pickers are shown for Roof, Wall Cladding, Trims, and Structure
Selecting a RAL swatch immediately updates the corresponding 3D material color
RAL code and name are displayed for each swatch
Selected colors are persisted to the backend project record

file_operations.description: Create a reusable RAL color picker component that displays a curated list of at least 20 common RAL codes with their hex equivalents as selectable color swatches. Include RAL codes like 7016 (Anthracite Grey), 9006 (White Aluminium), 3000 (Flame Red), 5010 (Gentian Blue), 6005 (Moss Green), etc. Display each swatch as a clickable button with the RAL code and name. Accept props: label (e.g., 'Roof Cladding'), selectedRAL (current RAL code), and onChange callback. Use shadcn/ui components (Button, Popover or Dialog for swatch grid). Return selected RAL code and hex value to parent.

Create a data module that exports a curated list of RAL colors as an array of objects with properties: code (string like 'RAL 7016'), name (string like 'Anthracite Grey'), and hex (string like '#2E3436'). Include at least 20 common RAL colors covering greys, blues, greens, reds, and neutrals.

Integrate four RALColorPicker instances into the BrandingControls panel, one each for Roof Cladding, Wall Cladding, Trims, and Structural Members. Add state for each RAL color selection. Display pickers in a grid or vertical stack layout. Emit color changes to BuildingSimulator to update 3D materials. Wire color selections to persist to the backend project brandingSettings record.

Accept RAL color props for roof, wall, trims, and structure. Apply corresponding hex colors to materials used in CladdingSystem, BuildingSignage, structural members (columns, rafters), and trim elements. Use Three.js Color instances to convert hex to material colors. Ensure color changes update materials in real-time.

requirements.summary: Implement first-person walkthrough mode with WASD movement and collision detection
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Enter Building Tour' locks the pointer and enables first-person movement
WASD keys move the camera forward/back/left/right inside the building
Mouse movement rotates the view while pointer is locked
Camera does not pass through wall or column geometries
Pressing Escape or clicking a toggle exits walkthrough and restores OrbitControls
Interior view starts at ground level looking toward the rafters

file_operations.description: Create a React Three Fiber component implementing first-person walkthrough mode using PointerLockControls from @react-three/drei. Listen for WASD key events (useEffect with keydown/keyup listeners) to move the camera forward, backward, left, and right relative to the look direction. Use useFrame to update camera position each frame based on active key states and movement speed. Implement basic collision detection using raycasting or bounding box checks against wall and column geometries: cast rays in movement direction and prevent position update if collision is detected. Accept props for building geometry bounds and collision meshes. Start camera at ground level inside the building looking upward toward the rafters. Expose a toggle to enable/disable pointer lock mode.

Add state for view mode toggle ('Orbit' or 'Interior'). Conditionally render either OrbitControls or WalkthroughController based on view mode state. Pass building geometry and collision mesh references to WalkthroughController. Ensure smooth transition between control modes without camera jump. Disable OrbitControls when walkthrough is active and vice versa.

Create a UI control component with a button labeled 'Enter Building Tour' (or 'Exit Tour' when active) that toggles between Orbit View and Interior View modes. Use shadcn/ui Button component. Emit view mode state to BuildingSimulator. Show a tooltip or help text explaining WASD + mouse controls when interior mode is active. Style with the industrial theme.

Wire the ViewModeToggle button into the ProjectViewer UI, positioning it prominently near the timeline controls. Ensure the toggle is easily accessible for switching between exterior orbit view and interior walkthrough.

requirements.summary: Implement client-side video export using MediaRecorder API
acceptanceCriteria: Export Video button is visible in the viewer controls
Clicking it plays the full erection sequence while recording the canvas
Recording uses MediaRecorder with a high bitrate codec (VP9 or VP8)
A WebM file is automatically downloaded when recording ends
Button shows a recording indicator while capture is in progress

file_operations.description: Create a custom React hook that manages video export using the MediaRecorder API. Accept a canvas element ref as input. Implement startRecording function that creates a MediaRecorder instance from the canvas stream with high bitrate settings (target 8–15 Mbps, use VP9 codec if available, fallback to VP8). Collect recorded chunks in an array. Implement stopRecording function that finalizes the recording, creates a Blob from chunks, generates a download URL, and triggers browser download of the WebM file with filename 'PEB-Sim-Export-{timestamp}.webm'. Return recording state (isRecording boolean) and start/stop functions. Handle errors gracefully and clean up resources.

Create a UI button component that triggers video export. Use the useVideoExport hook with the Three.js canvas ref. When clicked, start the erection sequence animation from step 0 to final step at a controlled playback speed, and simultaneously start recording. Show a recording indicator (red dot or progress text) while recording is in progress. Disable the button during recording. When the animation completes, stop recording and trigger download. Use shadcn/ui Button component with appropriate styling (red recording state). Handle errors with toast notifications.

Add an imperative handle or callback prop to control the erection sequence animation playback programmatically. Expose a playFullSequence function that animates from step 0 to the final step at a fixed speed and calls a completion callback when finished. Accept a canvas ref prop to pass to the VideoExportButton. Ensure the animation runs smoothly at consistent frame rate during recording.

Wire the VideoExportButton into the ProjectViewer UI, positioning it alongside other viewer controls (timeline, view mode toggle). Pass the canvas ref and animation control functions from BuildingSimulator to the VideoExportButton. Ensure the button is accessible and clearly labeled.

requirements.summary: Add Shadow Study feature with Time of Day slider and dynamic sun positioning
acceptanceCriteria: Time of Day slider is accessible in the UI panel
Moving the slider repositions the sun light and updates shadows in real-time
Shadows are cast on the ground plane and interior floor
When skylights are enabled, light patches are visible on the interior floor

file_operations.description: Create a React Three Fiber component that manages a DirectionalLight for the shadow study. Accept a timeOfDay prop (number from 6 to 20 representing hours). Calculate sun position based on time: map time to sun angle (low angle at 6:00 and 20:00, high angle at midday). Position the DirectionalLight at calculated spherical coordinates (radius, elevation, azimuth) to simulate sun movement across the sky. Enable castShadow on the DirectionalLight. Configure shadow camera frustum and resolution for high quality shadows. Update light position dynamically when timeOfDay prop changes.

Integrate the ShadowStudy component into the 3D scene. Add state for time of day (default to 12:00). Pass timeOfDay to ShadowStudy component. Enable castShadow and receiveShadow on all relevant meshes: structural members, cladding, ground plane, and interior floor. Configure the renderer to enable shadows (shadowMap.enabled = true). Ensure shadow updates are performant.

Create a UI control component with a slider input for Time of Day (range 6:00–20:00, step 0.5 hours). Display the current time value (formatted as HH:MM). Use shadcn/ui Slider component. Emit time changes to BuildingSimulator to update the sun position in real-time. Style with the industrial theme and add a sun icon for visual clarity.

Wire the ShadowStudyControls into the ProjectViewer UI, positioning it in the lighting or environment controls section. Ensure the slider is easily accessible and updates shadows smoothly without lag.

requirements.summary: Add Clearance Check tool with draggable vehicle presets and collision detection
acceptanceCriteria: Clearance Check toggle adds a draggable 3D box to the scene
User can select Truck or Forklift preset sizes
Box can be dragged along the floor plane with mouse/pointer
Box turns red when it intersects a structural member, green when clear
Clearance check is independent of walkthrough mode and works in orbit view

file_operations.description: Create a React Three Fiber component that renders a draggable 3D box representing a vehicle. Accept preset prop ('Truck' or 'Forklift') and set dimensions accordingly: Truck (6m L × 2.5m W × 3.5m H), Forklift (3m L × 1.5m W × 2.2m H). Render a BoxGeometry with the selected dimensions. Use useDrag hook from @use-gesture/react or pointer events to enable dragging along the XZ plane (floor). Implement collision detection using bounding box intersection checks against structural member geometries (columns, knee-braces): if the box intersects any member, set material color to red (#ff0000); otherwise set to green (#00ff00). Use MeshStandardMaterial with emissive color and some transparency. Update collision state and color on every drag event.

Add state for clearance check mode toggle and selected vehicle preset. Conditionally render the ClearanceCheckBox component when clearance check is enabled. Pass building structural member geometries (columns, knee-braces) as collision references. Position the box at a default starting location on the floor. Ensure the clearance check works in orbit view and does not interfere with walkthrough mode.

Create a UI control component with a toggle switch to enable/disable Clearance Check mode and a radio group or dropdown to select vehicle preset (Truck or Forklift). Display dimensions for the selected preset. Use shadcn/ui components (Switch, RadioGroup, Label). Emit toggle state and preset selection to BuildingSimulator. Style with the industrial theme.
Wire the ClearanceCheckControls into the ProjectViewer UI, positioning it in a tools or utilities section. Ensure the clearance check controls are accessible and clearly labeled.
